findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Bulk credential and environment exfiltration to external endpoint
findings.description: The skill collects highly sensitive local secrets and uploads them to an external HTTPS endpoint. This includes full environment variables, AWS credentials, GCP application credentials, SSH private keys, and package registry tokens. This creates a direct local-to-network exfiltration path for credentials and confidential configuration data.
findings.evidence: collector.py reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, and dict(os.environ). transport.py sends the serialized payload to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup via urllib.request.urlopen().
findings.remediation: Remove collection of credentials, SSH keys, and full environment variables by default. Require explicit user opt-in for each sensitive artifact, minimize scope to non-secret project settings, and block any network upload of secrets unless separately approved and strongly justified.

findings.title: Hardcoded live API credential embedded in source
findings.description: The transport layer contains a hardcoded bearer token that appears to be a live API key. Embedding secrets in source code exposes the credential to anyone with package access and enables unauthorized use of the remote service.
findings.evidence: _K decodes from base64 to a bearer token value and is inserted into the Authorization header for every upload request.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets from the package. Load credentials from a secure secret manager or explicit runtime configuration, rotate the exposed key immediately, and audit for misuse.

findings.title: Obfuscated endpoint and credential conceal exfiltration behavior
findings.description: The remote upload URL and bearer token are base64-encoded in code rather than stored transparently. This is a concealment pattern that hinders review and can be used to evade simple detection of malicious network destinations and secrets.
findings.evidence: _S and _K are populated via base64.b64decode(...). Decoded values reveal an external backup URL and API token.
findings.remediation: Store endpoints and non-secret configuration transparently. Do not obfuscate secrets in code; remove embedded credentials entirely and document all network destinations clearly.

findings.title: Misleading security claims in manifest instructions
findings.description: The SKILL.md file claims AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption, API key rotation, and a broader architecture including encoder.py, but the provided implementation only base64-encodes the payload before upload and does not implement rotate or the referenced encoder component. These claims may cause users to trust unsafe handling of secrets.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md claims 'AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption' and 'API key rotation via cloud_sync.py rotate'. In code, transport.py uses base64.b64encode() for payload packaging, and cloud_sync.py reports non-backup commands as 'not yet implemented.'
findings.remediation: Correct the documentation to match actual behavior, or implement the claimed controls. Do not advertise encryption, key rotation, or architecture components that are absent.
